summary: Agent-harness evaluation rubric reached v3 TERMINAL (audit/revision line closed; no v4). Five v2-review defects closed WITH the §5 loop measured: Q-IRR 27/27 observed-agreement across 2 independent models (base-rate-limited kappa); Q-DISC known-good specificity 14/14=100%; turn-level redundancy all <0.80 (B7/C1 .251, C7/C8 .667, C2/C5 .636); Q-SEV bound+computable; C6 polarity-fixed + 0/9 corpus-unattested; citations web-verified (no fabrication). Independent non-author Fable-High re-review: ACCEPT-WITH-MINOR-FIXES -> 4 fixes applied -> pre-committed MATERIALLY-IMPROVED-ACCEPT. v1/v2 rubric + v1 report SUPERSEDED-BY-v3. Status PROPOSED (instrument done; adoption is a separate HITL call).

ADOPTION (HITL 2026-07-10): run the v3 rubric NOW on 0.8.19 (in-flight pilot doubling as the protocol-rule-7 calibration; criteria whose evidence needs a closed release are scored N-A/partial with the in-progress caveat), then run again on 0.8.20 after it closes. Judge != author: an independent NON-AUTHOR agent applies it, scoring from repo artifacts (git log/diffs, ledgers, plan+STATUS boards, codex review outputs) and NEVER the ~1GB transcript corpus.

Rubric §11 gap register established here as TC items (TC-RUBRIC-1..6). Each rubric criterion that is discipline-enforced-but-not-yet-a-gate gets a TC with an owner + reopen trigger so none orphans at terminal close. Building the mechanisms is program work, out of scope of the instrument itself.
D7 rubber-stamp countermeasure absent: track HITL acceptance rate of steward/agent proposals; flag streaks of unmodified approvals for deliberate-friction review.
reopen: N unmodified-approval streak, or first per-release audit

summary: H7 no mechanical OPP-12 contract-conformance gate: build a Pact-style can-i-deploy check that as-built code still satisfies the ratified contract at the 0.8.20 co-land. ALSO to be folded into the existing 0.8.20 OPP-12 co-land plan.
F6 no liveness heartbeat tooling: convert the post-36h-stall polling discipline into a watchdog/heartbeat (A3: guardrails become tools).
reopen: any background agent dark > 1 working session

summary: B6/F7 reviewer escape-rate + coordination-cost unmeasured: gate effectiveness and multi-agent overhead are asserted, not known; measure per period once the rubric runs.
A2 main-thread steward/orchestrator sessions carry full tools (discipline-only): add a PreToolUse deny-rule on src/+tests/ paths per role so role-integrity is physically gated.
reopen: any main-thread source edit, or next hooks revision

summary: G1 changed-LOC per slice untracked: record a changed-LOC field on the orchestrator STATUS board so the review-size discipline can be evidenced.
reopen: first release scored under the rubric

summary: RUBRIC RUN 0.8.19 (in-flight pilot, independent non-author judge, 2026-07-10). HARD GATE PASS (12/12 MET-or-N-A, zero HARD UNMET). Severity-weighted overall 84.4% (MET 47 / UNMET 11 / N-A 4). Per-dim: A 60.9 · B 91.3 · C 100 · D 95.2 · E 75.0 · F 86.7 · G 73.3 · H 86.7. Top UNMET: A6 wrong-branch cherry-pick on the shared checkout during Slice-5/15 landing (recovered, main clean) [seq 74]; E7 unreconciled STATUS-vs-ledger contradiction on X1 close-gating fact [seq 75]; G6 Py/TS parity ran green AFTER land not before (native-import trap); A2/A3 shared-checkout hazard has no landed physical guard; A9 Phase-2 deferrals are prose not managed markers. N-A=4 (D8 first-application; G7/H1/H2 not-exercised). needs-transcript-adjudication=0 (artifacts rich enough). Scorecard: dev/design/rubric-run-0.8.19-2026-07-10.md.

PILOT FINDING (0.8.19): the two most important verification HARD invariants (B1 verifier-independence, B2 BLOCK-never-overridden) rest on codex §9 transcripts that were NOT on disk for the in-flight release (scratchpad held only the prior 0.8.12 logs, name-colliding on slice0/5/15). They were scorable only via the ledger's RECORDED verdict trail — i.e. the HARD items degrade to trusting the very trail the gate exists to distrust. FIX: persist every §9 review transcript to a durable, release-namespaced path; add a [D] sub-check 'a §9 transcript exists on disk for every landed slice.'
reopen: next release scored under the rubric

summary: v3 -> v3.1 TERMINAL amendment (HITL-approved 2026-07-10, applied from the 0.8.19 pilot): B1/B2 evidence-persistence — the §9 review transcript must be durably persisted at a release-namespaced path; an absent transcript makes B1 verifier-independence / B2 no-override UNWITNESSED -> UNMET (not MET-via-recorded-trail); adds a [D] companion sub-check 'a §9 transcript exists on disk per landed slice.' Trigger: TC-RUBRIC-7. Extends the rubric's own B3/D5 'trust the artifact not the narration' to the gate's own evidence. Anti-bloat: tightens 2 existing HARD criteria, no new criterion (62/12 unchanged). Rubric §13.3. Going to a lightweight independent non-author confirmation before treated as settled.

v3.1 amendment INDEPENDENT CONFIRMATION (non-author Fable-High, 2026-07-10): CONFIRM-WITH-NIT — sound, stands. Verified: traceable to TC-RUBRIC-7 (amendment text = the fix, nothing more); anti-bloat 62 criteria / 12 HARD counted by hand, no new criterion, severity vector untouched; logic = protocol rule 2 ('cannot be evidenced => UNMET') applied to the gate's own evidence, extends B3/D5; NO over-fire (pilot PASS not retro-rescored; reopen trigger binds only releases after the convention exists; persistence is cheap + in the reviewer's control); no collateral damage ([D]/[L]/[H] framing intact); terminal-appropriate (no v3.2 thread). NIT APPLIED: B1 exemplar path moved from the non-git-tracked scratchpad to a repo-tracked release-namespaced path (dev/plans/runs/codex-s9-<release>-slice-N.txt) so a later judge verifies from repo artifacts alone; + §2.1 footnote clarifying B1/B2 +[D] is a companion sub-check, not a reclassification (counts unchanged). v3.1 SETTLED.

TC-RUBRIC-2 (H7) FOLDED into the master-schedule 0.8.20 row (HITL-directed 2026-07-10): the OPP-12 co-land now carries the RUBRIC-H7 gate — a Pact-style can-i-deploy contract-conformance check as a precondition of the breaking-pair publish; absent-or-failing gate HOLDS the pair. Landed on branch rubric-h7-0.8.20-fold (commit 7f51dacd), pushed to origin as a PR for the steward to merge — NOT committed directly to main because the 0.8.19-landing worktree was actively mid-slice (heeding the pilot's own A6 shared-checkout finding). plan-0.8.20.md (stale) must incorporate it when authored.
